heading: Guaranteed renewability of individual health insurance coverage
label: 300gg–42 Guaranteed renewability of individual health insurance coverage
notes: Effective DateSection applicable with respect to health insurance coverage offered, sold, issued, renewed, in effect, or operated in the individual market after , regardless of when a period of creditable coverage occurs, see , set out as a note under .June 30, 1997section 111(b) of Pub. L. 104–191section 300gg–41 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Except as provided in this section, a health insurance issuer that provides individual health insurance coverage to an individual shall renew or continue in force such coverage at the option of the individual.

children.heading: Nonpayment of premiums
children.label: 1 Nonpayment of premiums
children.text: The individual has failed to pay premiums or contributions in accordance with the terms of the health insurance coverage or the issuer has not received timely premium payments.

children.heading: Fraud
children.label: 2 Fraud
children.text: The individual has performed an act or practice that constitutes fraud or made an intentional misrepresentation of material fact under the terms of the coverage.

children.heading: Termination of plan
children.label: 3 Termination of plan
children.text: The issuer is ceasing to offer coverage in the individual market in accordance with subsection (c) and applicable State law.

children.heading: Movement outside service area
children.label: 4 Movement outside service area
children.text: In the case of a health insurance issuer that offers health insurance coverage in the market through a network plan, the individual no longer resides, lives, or works in the service area (or in an area for which the issuer is authorized to do business) but only if such coverage is terminated under this paragraph uniformly without regard to any health status-related factor of covered individuals.

children.heading: Association membership ceases
children.label: 5 Association membership ceases
children.text: In the case of health insurance coverage that is made available in the individual market only through one or more bona fide associations, the membership of the individual in the association (on the basis of which the coverage is provided) ceases but only if such coverage is terminated under this paragraph uniformly without regard to any health status-related factor of covered individuals.

children.heading: Requirements for uniform termination of coverage
children.label: c Requirements for uniform termination of coverage

children.heading: Particular type of coverage not offered
children.label: 1 Particular type of coverage not offered
A
children.text: the issuer provides notice to each covered individual provided coverage of this type in such market of such discontinuation at least 90 days prior to the date of the discontinuation of such coverage;

children.label: B
children.text: the issuer offers to each individual in the individual market provided coverage of this type, the option to purchase any other individual health insurance coverage currently being offered by the issuer for individuals in such market; and

children.label: C
children.text: in exercising the option to discontinue coverage of this type and in offering the option of coverage under subparagraph (B), the issuer acts uniformly without regard to any health status-related factor of enrolled individuals or individuals who may become eligible for such coverage.

children.label: i
children.text: the issuer provides notice to the applicable State authority and to each individual of such discontinuation at least 180 days prior to the date of the expiration of such coverage, and

children.label: ii
children.text: all health insurance issued or delivered for issuance in the State in such market are discontinued and coverage under such health insurance coverage in such market is not renewed.

children.heading: Prohibition on market reentry
children.label: B Prohibition on market reentry
children.text: In the case of a discontinuation under subparagraph (A) in the individual market, the issuer may not provide for the issuance of any health insurance coverage in the market and State involved during the 5-year period beginning on the date of the discontinuation of the last health insurance coverage not so renewed.

children.heading: Exception for uniform modification of coverage
children.label: d Exception for uniform modification of coverage
children.text: At the time of coverage renewal, a health insurance issuer may modify the health insurance coverage for a policy form offered to individuals in the individual market so long as such modification is consistent with State law and effective on a uniform basis among all individuals with that policy form.

children.heading: Application to coverage offered only through associations
children.label: e Application to coverage offered only through associations
children.text: In applying this section in the case of health insurance coverage that is made available by a health insurance issuer in the individual market to individuals only through one or more associations, a reference to an “individual” is deemed to include a reference to such an association (of which the individual is a member).
